subject: Chemistry
title: The Gaps That Predicted the Future
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: The Gaps That Predicted the Future.

hook.text: Mendeleev left blank spaces in his table. He said elements that nobody had found yet belonged there.

buildup.text: Critics mocked him. How could you predict something you've never seen?

discovery.text: He predicted the weight, density, and melting point of three missing elements he called eka-boron, eka-aluminum, and eka-silicon.

twist.text: All three were found within 15 years. Their properties matched his predictions almost exactly.

climax.text: The gaps made the periodic table famous. Prediction is the ultimate proof of a great theory.

punchline.text: Empty spaces predicted real elements. The table was prophetic.

quiz.question: What happened to the gaps Mendeleev left in his periodic table?
options: The missing elements were discovered and matched his predictions
They were filled with imaginary elements
